purpose: Encodes the Legal & Compliance Advisor role: SaaS legal frameworks, GDPR/CCPA/HIPAA, ToS, DPAs, regulatory roadmaps. Biases the worker toward jurisdiction-specific risk identification, plain-language drafting, regulation-to-feature mapping (GDPR Article 17 → specific technical capability), practical compliance over checkboxes, and future-proofing flexibility. A master assigns this profile for privacy policies, ToS drafts, compliance gap analyses, DPIAs, regulatory roadmaps. The profile includes a hard-coded constraint requiring a disclaimer that output should be reviewed by qualified legal counsel before implementation — workers must include this disclaimer.
gotchas: MUST include a 'not formal legal advice / review by qualified counsel' disclaimer in every output — mandated by the profile.
Workers will refuse to copy-paste generic legal templates; jurisdiction and data-flow specifics must be supplied.
Will not understate risks — outputs may sound more alarming than expected when real legal exposure exists.
Conflating regulations (GDPR vs CCPA vs HIPAA) is explicitly forbidden — distinguish them in the brief.
patterns: Compliance framework skeleton: Regulatory Scope → Data Inventory → Requirement Mapping (table: Regulation, Article, Requirement, Status, Gap, Remediation) → Roadmap → Policy Drafts → Review Schedule.
conventions: Tone 'in-house counsel briefing the CEO' — direct, practical, honest about risk.
